text.title: Enterprise XR Applications Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the enterprise-xr-applications methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Use case MUST have >=50% headset coverage in the target population OR a documented procurement plan to reach 50% within 6 months.
rule.rationale: XR-only flows shipped to <50% coverage strand half the workforce; the gate prevents shelfware.

rule.statement: Long-session use cases (>=30 min) MUST declare a comfort budget: max continuous time, mandated break cadence, vestibular-safe motion patterns.
rule.rationale: Without comfort budget, fatigue causes abandonment + workplace safety risk.

rule.statement: Spec MUST list IT integration points: identity (SSO), MDM, asset management, network requirements (latency/bandwidth), offline mode.
rule.rationale: Enterprise XR rollout stalls on IT integration; surfacing it early prevents launch delays.

rule.statement: Spec MUST pass a security review covering: spatial data privacy, camera/mic permissions, cloud session data residency, recording governance.
rule.rationale: Enterprise XR captures more sensitive context than 2D apps; security review is mandatory.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare ROI baseline + target + measurement method (cost savings, throughput, error reduction); ROI-less XR pilots MUST NOT proceed past pilot.
rule.rationale: Without ROI gate, enterprise pilots stall in indefinite pilot phase.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
